name: Nephrotic-range proteinuria spot urine protein/creatinine (U p/c) cutoff: pediatric sources (>2 mg/mg) vs. a general/adult-scoped narrative-review source (>350 mg/mmol = 3.5 mg/mg)

contributingSources.claimText: Nephrotic-range (heavy) proteinuria is defined as more than 1,000 mg per m2 per day, or a urine protein-to-creatinine ratio of more than 2.
contributingSources.locator: Nephrotic syndrome paragraph ("Nephrotic syndrome is characterized by heavy proteinuria...")

contributingSources.claimText: Nephrotic-range proteinuria is defined as >1,000 mg/m2 per day, or >50 mg/kg per day, or a spot U p/c >2.
A spot urine P/C ratio above 350 mg/mmol (3.5 mg/mg) confirms nephrotic-range proteinuria.

conflictBasis: clm_inf02 (inference, confidence high, from_claims=[clm_004, clm_029, clm_015]): clm_004 and clm_029 (both pediatric-scoped, cited by src_20260718_rfkid001_07 and src_20260718_rfkid001_06 respectively) each independently give the nephrotic-range spot urine protein/creatinine ratio cutoff as greater than 2 mg/mg. clm_015 (a general/adult-scoped narrative review, src_20260718_rfkid001_10) instead gives greater than 350 mg/mmol (3.5 mg/mg). Unit conversion (1 mg/mg protein/creatinine ≈ 113 mg/mmol) shows 2 mg/mg ≈ 226 mg/mmol, which is materially LOWER than 350 mg/mmol -- a genuine discordance between the pediatric and general/adult cutoffs, not a rounding artifact or a unit-conversion error.
resolutionNote: This conflict is preserved here as an explicit, named, multi-source object per FR-11/FR-12 (evidence-foundry seam invariant 8) -- it is never averaged, never collapsed to a single source, and never silently resolved by this converter or by any script in this pass. Selecting one cutoff over the other (or defining an age/context- dependent branch between them) is a clinical rule-authoring decision that requires an approved modules/kidney_suite_v1/authoring-decisions.yaml record and independent clinical review; none exists yet (Deferred Item DF-E1-M1). A consumer resolving this conflict by any of clm_inf02/clm_004/clm_029/clm_015 must surface every contributing source listed here, never a single winner.
reason: Nephrotic-range proteinuria has two source-derived spot U p/c cutoffs that materially disagree (pediatric vs. general/adult) and, per FR-11/FR-12, must not be reconciled by this converter -- this record exists so any consumer querying by claim_id clm_inf02, clm_004, clm_029, or clm_015 resolves to this same named, multi-source conflict object.
